task_completion.description: The 8 missing variables were added to the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagram in draw.io or the final PDF shows nodes for 'Attractiveness of Driving', 'Road Expansion Budget', 'Remote Work Adoption', 'Air Quality', 'Fuel Consumption', 'CO2 Emissions', 'Population Growth', and 'Housing Demand Near Transit'.

task_completion.description: Causal links between variables have polarity labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Arrows connecting the variable nodes have text labels showing '+' or '-' near their arrowheads or along the lines.

task_completion.description: The 5 required feedback loops are annotated on the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagram contains text or circular arrow annotations explicitly labeled R1, R2, B1, B2, and B3, with text identifying them as Reinforcing (R) or Balancing (B).

task_completion.description: A legend explaining the notation is included in the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct box or section labeled 'Legend' (or similar) is present, explaining the meaning of the '+' and '-' signs and/or the loop annotations.

task_completion.description: The original draw.io file was successfully edited and completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io GUI shows the completed diagram with all required elements, and the window title or tab indicates 'traffic_congestion_cld.drawio'.

task_completion.description: The final diagram was exported as a PDF to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'traffic_congestion_cld.pdf' is visible in the '~/Diagrams/' directory, either shown in a file manager window, a terminal listing (ls), or opened in a PDF viewer.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to edit the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io (diagrams.net) application interface being used to drag, drop, and connect nodes, rather than a text editor modifying the raw XML.

integrity.description: The PDF was exported using the application's native export feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent using the draw.io menu (File -> Export as -> PDF) or a print dialog, rather than using a command-line conversion tool.

integrity.description: The diagram consists of genuine draw.io elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram should look like a standard draw.io flowchart with selectable nodes and edges, not a single pasted image of a pre-made diagram.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial diagram has 6 nodes. The completed diagram must have 14 nodes total. The 8 required new variables are: Attractiveness of Driving, Road Expansion Budget, Remote Work Adoption, Air Quality, Fuel Consumption, CO2 Emissions, Population Growth, and Housing Demand Near Transit. The diagram must include exactly 5 required feedback loops labeled R1, R2, B1, B2, B3. The final PDF must be located at ~/Diagrams/traffic_congestion_cld.pdf.
